symptoms.symptom_name: Acute severe generalized pain flare on a background of post-MVA chronic widespread pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Escalating widespread pain triggered by minor exertion on a post-trauma background fits a central sensitization flare.
Truly generalized whole-body pain is not the pattern of a focal vertebral fracture.

symptoms.symptom_name: Generalized allodynia/hyperalgesia to light touch in all quadrants

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Four-quadrant allodynia is a hallmark of central pain amplification.

symptoms.symptom_name: Paraspinal muscle spasm with pain-limited spinal range of motion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Protective muscle guarding accompanies central pain flares.
Spasm can accompany fracture but is non-specific; focal bony tenderness would be needed.

symptoms.symptom_name: No bowel/bladder incontinence, no saddle anaesthesia, no new leg weakness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of sphincter disturbance, saddle anaesthesia and leg weakness makes cauda equina very unlikely, but it must be explicitly screened.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sensory hypervigilance to light/noise with panic

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Central sensitization commonly extends to sensory hypervigilance.
Hypervigilance and panic reflect comorbid post-traumatic anxiety amplifying the flare.

symptoms.symptom_name: Failure of maximal OTC NSAID dose to relieve pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Poor response to peripheral anti-inflammatories is typical of centralized pain and signals NSAID overuse risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal urine colour and output (no visible myoglobinuria)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal urine colour/output makes significant rhabdomyolysis less likely, though CK is required to exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: No focal bony spinal point tenderness or palpable step-off (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of focal bony tenderness/step-off on exam argues against vertebral fracture.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intact motor strength and normal reflexes (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal neuro exam argues against cord/cauda equina compression.

symptoms.symptom_name: Creatine kinase (CK) — expected normal/only mildly raised

loinc_ref.display: Creatine kinase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma

investigation_details.expected_result: Normal or only mildly elevated; a markedly high CK would indicate rhabdomyolysis requiring urgent care.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cannot definitively exclude rhabdomyolysis without CK; obtain via in-person pathway if any doubt.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: CK is the definitive test to confirm or exclude rhabdomyolysis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Prior motor vehicle accident with post-traumatic chronic pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Physical trauma is a recognised trigger for post-traumatic central sensitization/fibromyalgia.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Four-quadrant allodynia, protective spasm, sensory hypervigilance and NSAID-unresponsive pain, with a clean neurological screen and normal urine, constitute a post-traumatic centralized pain flare rather than a new structural or muscle-breakdown emergency.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person neurological examination (power, reflexes, saddle sensation, anal tone if indicated)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Required to exclude cauda equina/cord compression, which cannot be assessed via chat.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum creatine kinase (and urine dipstick for blood/myoglobin)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude rhabdomyolysis if any doubt, particularly given severe muscle pain.
Not indicated without focal bony tenderness or neurological deficit; would over-investigate a central pain flare.
